name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **PAUSE LIKELY REDUCES AWRP RE-SPIKE RISK; VERIFICATION FRAMEWORK REFRAMED.** Yesterday's brief had 'AWRP 5%+ within 5 sessions' as the verification trigger for Project Freedom effectiveness. With Project Freedom paused after one day (May 5) and the diplomatic track re-opened, the structural insurance-pricing pressure has likely eased, NOT intensified. Latest hard data: Argus Media confirms AWRP at ~1% Hull and Machinery value per seven-day period (down from 2.5-5% peak); VLCC TD3C remains at ~$420-474K/day (Lloyd's List). New verification framework: AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions = pause being priced as durable de-escalation; AWRP staying at 0.8-1.5% baseline = market pricing pause as fragile; AWRP re-spiking to 5%+ = pause framework breaking. Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED — operational chokepoint persists despite kinetic-war probability decline. The market's tanker-paradox confirms this duality: Frontline +2.8%, BOAT +3.5% on May 6 despite Brent collapse — physical Hormuz blockade NOT resolved, only kinetic-war probability compressed. P&I Club re-pricing typically lags 3-7 sessions; watch May 7-12 for the cleanest read on whether insurers price the pause as durable or fragile.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.
